◆ convertNameToFile()

QCString convertNameToFile ( const QCString & name,
bool allowDots,
bool allowUnderscore )

This function determines the file name on disk of an item given its name, which could be a class name with template arguments, so special characters need to be escaped.

Definition at line 3842 of file util.cpp.

3843{
3844 if (name.isEmpty()) return name;
3845 bool shortNames = Config_getBool(SHORT_NAMES);
3846 bool createSubdirs = Config_getBool(CREATE_SUBDIRS);
3847 QCString result;
3848 if (shortNames) // use short names only
3849 {
3850 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(g_usedNamesMutex);
3851 auto kv = g_usedNames.find(name.str());
3852 uint32_t num=0;
3853 if (kv!=g_usedNames.end())
3854 {
3855 num = kv->second;
3856 }
3857 else
3858 {
3859 num = g_usedNamesCount;
3860 g_usedNames.insert(std::make_pair(name.str(),g_usedNamesCount++));
3861 }
3862 result.sprintf("a%05d",num);
3863 }
3864 else // long names
3865 {
3866 result=escapeCharsInString(name,allowDots,allowUnderscore);
3867 size_t resultLen = result.length();
3868 if (resultLen>=128) // prevent names that cannot be created!
3869 {
3870 // third algorithm based on MD5 hash
3871 uint8_t md5_sig[16];
3872 char sigStr[33];
3873 MD5Buffer(result.data(),static_cast<unsigned int>(resultLen),md5_sig);
3874 MD5SigToString(md5_sig,sigStr);
3875 result=result.left(128-32)+sigStr;
3876 }
3877 }
3878 if (createSubdirs)
3879 {
3880 int l1Dir=0,l2Dir=0;
3881 int createSubdirsLevel = Config_getInt(CREATE_SUBDIRS_LEVEL);
3882 int createSubdirsBitmaskL2 = (1<<createSubdirsLevel)-1;
3883
3884 // compute md5 hash to determine sub directory to use
3885 uint8_t md5_sig[16];
3886 MD5Buffer(result.data(),static_cast<unsigned int>(result.length()),md5_sig);
3887 l1Dir = md5_sig[14] & 0xf;
3888 l2Dir = md5_sig[15] & createSubdirsBitmaskL2;
3889
3890 result.prepend(QCString().sprintf("d%x/d%02x/",l1Dir,l2Dir));
3891 }
3892 //printf("*** convertNameToFile(%s)->%s\n",qPrint(name),qPrint(result));
3893 return result;
3894}

◆ convertProtectionLevel()

void convertProtectionLevel ( MemberListType inListType,
Protection inProt,
int * outListType1,
int * outListType2 )

Computes for a given list type inListType, which are the the corresponding list type(s) in the base class that are to be added to this list.

So for public inheritance, the mapping is 1-1, so outListType1=inListType Private members are to be hidden completely.

For protected inheritance, both protected and public members of the base class should be joined in the protected member section.

For private inheritance, both protected and public members of the base class should be joined in the private member section.

Definition at line 6484 of file util.cpp.

6490{
6491 bool extractPrivate = Config_getBool(EXTRACT_PRIVATE);
6492 // default representing 1-1 mapping
6493 *outListType1=inListType;
6494 *outListType2=-1;
6495 if (inProt==Protection::Public)
6496 {
6497 switch (inListType) // in the private section of the derived class,
6498 // the private section of the base class should not
6499 // be visible
6500 {
6501 case MemberListType_priMethods:
6502 case MemberListType_priStaticMethods:
6503 case MemberListType_priSlots:
6504 case MemberListType_priAttribs:
6505 case MemberListType_priStaticAttribs:
6506 case MemberListType_priTypes:
6507 *outListType1=-1;
6508 *outListType2=-1;
6509 break;
6510 default:
6511 break;
6512 }
6513 }
6514 else if (inProt==Protection::Protected) // Protected inheritance
6515 {
6516 switch (inListType) // in the protected section of the derived class,
6517 // both the public and protected members are shown
6518 // as protected
6519 {
6520 case MemberListType_pubMethods:
6521 case MemberListType_pubStaticMethods:
6522 case MemberListType_pubSlots:
6523 case MemberListType_pubAttribs:
6524 case MemberListType_pubStaticAttribs:
6525 case MemberListType_pubTypes:
6526 case MemberListType_priMethods:
6527 case MemberListType_priStaticMethods:
6528 case MemberListType_priSlots:
6529 case MemberListType_priAttribs:
6530 case MemberListType_priStaticAttribs:
6531 case MemberListType_priTypes:
6532 *outListType1=-1;
6533 *outListType2=-1;
6534 break;
6535
6536 case MemberListType_proMethods:
6537 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubMethods;
6538 break;
6539 case MemberListType_proStaticMethods:
6540 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubStaticMethods;
6541 break;
6542 case MemberListType_proSlots:
6543 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubSlots;
6544 break;
6545 case MemberListType_proAttribs:
6546 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubAttribs;
6547 break;
6548 case MemberListType_proStaticAttribs:
6549 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubStaticAttribs;
6550 break;
6551 case MemberListType_proTypes:
6552 *outListType2=MemberListType_pubTypes;
6553 break;
6554 default:
6555 break;
6556 }
6557 }
6558 else if (inProt==Protection::Private)
6559 {
6560 switch (inListType) // in the private section of the derived class,
6561 // both the public and protected members are shown
6562 // as private
6563 {
6564 case MemberListType_pubMethods:
6565 case MemberListType_pubStaticMethods:
6566 case MemberListType_pubSlots:
6567 case MemberListType_pubAttribs:
6568 case MemberListType_pubStaticAttribs:
6569 case MemberListType_pubTypes:
6570 case MemberListType_proMethods:
6571 case MemberListType_proStaticMethods:
6572 case MemberListType_proSlots:
6573 case MemberListType_proAttribs:
6574 case MemberListType_proStaticAttribs:
6575 case MemberListType_proTypes:
6576 *outListType1=-1;
6577 *outListType2=-1;
6578 break;
6579
6580 case MemberListType_priMethods:
6581 if (extractPrivate)
6582 {
6583 *outListType1=MemberListType_pubMethods;
6584 *outListType2=MemberListType_proMethods;
6585 }

6592 case MemberListType_priStaticMethods:

6596 *outListType2=MemberListType_proStaticMethods;
6597 }
6598 else
6599 {
6600 *outListType1=-1;
6601 *outListType2=-1;
6602 }
6603 break;
6604 case MemberListType_priSlots:
6605 if (extractPrivate)
6606 {
6607 *outListType1=MemberListType_pubSlots;
6608 *outListType2=MemberListType_proSlots;
6609 }
6610 else
6611 {
6612 *outListType1=-1;

7098{

7103 size_t i=0;
7104 int col=0;
7105 constexpr auto doxy_nbsp = "&_doxy_nbsp;"; // doxygen escape command for UTF-8 nbsp
7106 const int maxIndent=1000000; // value representing infinity
7107 int minIndent=maxIndent;
7108 while (i<size)
7109 {
7110 char c = data[i++];
7111 switch(c)
7112 {
7113 case '\t': // expand tab
7114 {
7115 int stop = tabSize - (col%tabSize);
7116 //printf("expand at %d stop=%d\n",col,stop);
7117 col+=stop;
7118 while (stop--) out.addChar(' ');
7119 }
7120 break;
7121 case '\n': // reset column counter
7122 out.addChar(c);
7123 col=0;
7124 break;
7125 case ' ': // increment column counter
7126 out.addChar(c);
7127 col++;
7128 break;
7129 default: // non-whitespace => update minIndent
7130 if (c<0 && i<size) // multibyte sequence
7131 {
7132 // special handling of the UTF-8 nbsp character 0xC2 0xA0
7133 int nb = isUTF8NonBreakableSpace(data);
7134 if (nb>0)
7135 {
7136 out.addStr(doxy_nbsp);
7137 i+=nb-1;
7138 }
7139 else
7140 {
7141 int bytes = getUTF8CharNumBytes(c);
7142 for (int j=0;j<bytes-1 && c;j++)
7143 {
7144 out.addChar(c);
7145 c = data[i++];
7146 }
7147 out.addChar(c);
7148 }
7149 }
7150 else
7151 {
7152 out.addChar(c);
7153 }
7154 if (col<minIndent) minIndent=col;
7155 col++;
7156 }
7157 }
7158 if (minIndent!=maxIndent) refIndent=minIndent; else refIndent=0;
7159 out.addChar(0);
7160 return out.get();
7161}

int & pos,

Extracts a (sub-)string from type starting at pos that could form a class. The index of the match is returned and the found class name and a template argument list templSpec. If -1 is returned there are no more matches.

4535
4536 name.clear();
4537 templSpec.clear();
4538 if (type.isEmpty()) return -1;
4539 size_t typeLen=type.length();

4541 {

4546 {

4552 reg::Iterator end;

4554 if (it!=end)
4555 {

4579 te++;

4581 }
4582 name = match.str();

4586 tl+=te-ts;

4591 pos=static_cast<int>(i+l);
4592 }

◆ extractDirection()

QCString extractDirection ( QCString & docs)

Strip the direction part from docs and return it as a string in canonical form The input docs string can start with e.g.

"[in]", "[in, out]", "[inout]", "[out,in]"...

Returns
either "[in,out]", "[in]", or "[out]" or the empty string.

6435{
6436 std::string s = docs.str();
6437 static const reg::Ex re(R"(\[([ inout,]+)\])");
6438 reg::Iterator it(s,re);
6439 reg::Iterator end;
6440 if (it!=end)
6441 {
6442 const auto &match = *it;
6443 size_t p = match.position();
6444 size_t l = match.length();
6445 if (p==0 && l>2)
6446 {
6447 // make dir the part inside [...] without separators
6448 std::string dir = match[1].str();
6449 // strip , and ' ' from dir
6450 dir.erase(std::remove_if(dir.begin(),dir.end(),
6451 [](const char c) { return c==' ' || c==','; }
6452 ),dir.end());
6453 size_t inIndex, outIndex;
6454 unsigned char ioMask=0;
6455 if (( inIndex=dir.find( "in"))!=std::string::npos) dir.erase( inIndex,2),ioMask|=(1<<0);
6456 if ((outIndex=dir.find("out"))!=std::string::npos) dir.erase(outIndex,3),ioMask|=(1<<1);
6457 if (dir.empty() && ioMask!=0) // only in and/or out attributes found
6458 {
6459 docs = s.substr(l); // strip attributes
6460 if (ioMask==((1<<0)|(1<<1))) return "[in,out]";
6461 else if (ioMask==(1<<0)) return "[in]";
6462 else if (ioMask==(1<<1)) return "[out]";

6464 }
6465 }
6466 return "";

◆ filterCRLF()

static void filterCRLF ( std::string & contents)
static

takes the buf of the given length len and converts CR LF (DOS) or CR (MAC) line ending to LF (Unix). Returns the length of the converted content (i.e. the same as len (Unix, MAC) or smaller (DOS).

Definition at line 1259 of file util.cpp.

1260{
1261 size_t src = 0; // source index
1262 size_t dest = 0; // destination index
1263 size_t len = contents.length();
1264
1265 while (src<len)
1266 {
1267 char c = contents[src++]; // Remember the processed character.
1268 if (c == '\r') // CR to be solved (MAC, DOS)
1269 {
1270 c = '\n'; // each CR to LF
1271 if (src<len && contents[src] == '\n')
1272 {
1273 ++src; // skip LF just after CR (DOS)
1274 }
1275 }
1276 else if ( c == '\0' && src<len-1) // filter out internal \0 characters, as it will confuse the parser
1277 {
1278 c = ' '; // turn into a space
1279 }
1280 contents[dest++] = c; // copy the (modified) character to dest
1281 }
1282 contents.resize(dest);
1283}

3180 QCString anchor,linkText=linkToText(SrcLangExt::Unknown,lt,FALSE);
3181 //printf("generateLink linkText=%s\n",qPrint(linkText));
3182 if (resolveLink(clName,lr,inSeeBlock,&compound,anchor))
3183 {
3184 if (compound) // link to compound
3185 {
3186 if (lt.isEmpty() && anchor.isEmpty() && /* compound link */
3187 compound->definitionType()==Definition::TypeGroup /* is group */
3188 )
3189 {
3190 linkText=(toGroupDef(compound))->groupTitle(); // use group's title as link
3191 }
3192 else if (compound->definitionType()==Definition::TypeFile)
3193 {
3194 linkText=linkToText(compound->getLanguage(),lt,TRUE);
3195 }
3196 ol.writeObjectLink(compound->getReference(),
3197 compound->getOutputFileBase(),anchor,linkText);
3198 if (!compound->isReference())
3199 {
3200 writePageRef(ol,compound->getOutputFileBase(),anchor);
3201 }
3202 }
3203 else
3204 {
3205 err("%s:%d: Internal error: resolveLink successful but no compound found!\n",__FILE__,__LINE__);
3206 }
3207 return TRUE;
3208 }
3209 else // link could not be found
3210 {
3211 ol.docify(linkText);
3212 return FALSE;
3213 }
3214}

◆ getDefsOld()

GetDefResult getDefsOld ( const GetDefInput & input)

Searches for a member definition given its name 'memberName' as a string. memberName may also include a (partial) scope to indicate the scope in which the member is located.

The parameter 'scName' is a string representing the name of the scope in which the link was found.

In case of a function args contains a string representation of the argument list. Passing 0 means the member has no arguments. Passing "()" means any argument list will do, but "()" is preferred.

The function returns TRUE if the member is known and documented or FALSE if it is not. If TRUE is returned parameter 'md' contains a pointer to the member definition. Furthermore exactly one of the parameter 'cd', 'nd', or 'fd' will be non-zero:

  • if 'cd' is non zero, the member was found in a class pointed to by cd.
  • if 'nd' is non zero, the member was found in a namespace pointed to by nd.
  • if 'fd' is non zero, the member was found in the global namespace of file fd.

Definition at line 2235 of file util.cpp.

2237 GetDefResult result;
2238 QCString scopeName;

2252 // qPrint(memberName),qPrint(args),qPrint(scopeName),forceEmptyScope);

2264 // qPrint(scopeName), qPrint(memberName));
2265
2266 mName=memberName;

2268 // as a special case
2269 (im=memberName.findRev("::"))!=-1 &&

2271 )
2272 {
2273 mScope=memberName.left(im);
2274 mName=memberName.right(memberName.length()-im-2);
2275 }
2276
2277 // handle special the case where both scope name and member scope are equal
2278 if (mScope==scopeName) scopeName.clear();
2279
2280 //printf("mScope='%s' mName='%s'\n",qPrint(mScope),qPrint(mName));
2281
2282 mn = Doxygen::memberNameLinkedMap->find(mName);
2283 //printf("mName=%s mn=%p\n",qPrint(mName),mn);
2284
2285 if ((!input.forceEmptyScope || input.scopeName.isEmpty()) && // this was changed for bug638856, forceEmptyScope => empty scopeName
2286 mn && !(input.scopeName.isEmpty() && mScope.isEmpty()))
2287 {
2288 //printf(" >member name '%s' found\n",qPrint(mName));
2289 int scopeOffset = static_cast<int>(scopeName.length());
2290 do
2291 {
2292 QCString className = scopeName.left(scopeOffset);
2293 if (!className.isEmpty() && !mScope.isEmpty())
2294 {
2295 className+="::"+mScope;
2296 }
2297 else if (!mScope.isEmpty())
2298 {
2299 className=mScope;
2300 }
2301
2302 SymbolResolver resolver;
2303 const ClassDef *fcd=resolver.resolveClass(Doxygen::globalScope,className);
2304 const MemberDef *tmd=resolver.getTypedef();
2305
2306 if (fcd==nullptr && className.find('<')!=-1) // try without template specifiers as well
2307 {
2308 QCString nameWithoutTemplates = stripTemplateSpecifiersFromScope(className,FALSE);
2309 fcd=resolver.resolveClass(Doxygen::globalScope,nameWithoutTemplates);
2310 tmd=resolver.getTypedef();
2311 }
2312 //printf("Trying class scope %s: fcd=%p tmd=%p\n",qPrint(className),fcd,tmd);
2313 // todo: fill in correct fileScope!
2314 if (fcd && // is it a documented class
2315 fcd->isLinkable()
2316 )
2317 {
2318 //printf(" Found fcd=%p\n",fcd);
2319 int mdist=maxInheritanceDepth;
2320 std::unique_ptr<ArgumentList> argList;
2321 if (!input.args.isEmpty())
2322 {
2323 argList = stringToArgumentList(fcd->getLanguage(),input.args);
2324 }
2325 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2326 {
2327 MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2328 if (!mmd->isStrongEnumValue())
2329 {
2330 const ArgumentList &mmdAl = mmd->argumentList();
2331 bool match = input.args.isEmpty() ||
2332 matchArguments2(mmd->getOuterScope(),mmd->getFileDef(),&mmdAl,
2333 fcd, fcd->getFileDef(),argList.get(),
2334 input.checkCV,mmd->getLanguage());
2335 //printf("match=%d\n",match);
2336 if (match)
2337 {
2338 const ClassDef *mcd=mmd->getClassDef();
2339 if (mcd)
2340 {
2341 int m=minClassDistance(fcd,mcd);
2342 if (m<mdist && mcd->isLinkable())
2343 {
2344 mdist=m;
2345 result.cd=mcd;
2346 result.md=mmd;
2347 }
2348 }
2349 }
2350 }
2351 }
2352 if (mdist==maxInheritanceDepth && input.args=="()")
2353 // no exact match found, but if args="()" an arbitrary member will do
2354 {
2355 //printf(" >Searching for arbitrary member\n");
2356 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2357 {
2358 MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2359 //if (mmd->isLinkable())
2360 //{
2361 const ClassDef *mcd=mmd->getClassDef();
2362 //printf(" >Class %s found\n",qPrint(mcd->name()));
2363 if (mcd)
2364 {
2365 int m=minClassDistance(fcd,mcd);
2366 if (m<mdist /* && mcd->isLinkable()*/ )
2367 {
2368 //printf("Class distance %d\n",m);
2369 mdist = m;
2370 result.cd = mcd;
2371 result.md = mmd;
2372 }
2373 }
2374 //}
2375 }
2376 }
2377 //printf(" >Success=%d\n",mdist<maxInheritanceDepth);
2378 if (mdist<maxInheritanceDepth)
2379 {
2380 if (!result.md->isLinkable() || result.md->isStrongEnumValue())
2381 {
2382 result.md = nullptr; // avoid returning things we cannot link to
2383 result.cd = nullptr;
2384 result.found = false; // match found, but was not linkable
2385 goto exit;
2386 }
2387 else
2388 {
2389 result.gd = result.md->getGroupDef();
2390 if (result.gd) result.cd=nullptr;
2391 result.found=true; /* found match */
2392 goto exit;
2393 }
2394 }
2395 }
2396 if (tmd && tmd->isEnumerate() && tmd->isStrong()) // scoped enum
2397 {
2398 //printf("Found scoped enum!\n");
2399 for (const auto &emd : tmd->enumFieldList())
2400 {
2401 if (emd->localName()==mName)
2402 {
2403 if (emd->isLinkable())
2404 {
2405 result.cd = tmd->getClassDef();
2406 result.md = emd;
2407 result.found = true;
2408 goto exit;
2409 }
2410 else
2411 {
2412 result.cd = nullptr;
2413 result.md = nullptr;
2414 result.found = false;
2415 goto exit;
2416 }
2417 }
2418 }
2419 }
2420 /* go to the parent scope */
2421 if (scopeOffset==0)
2422 {
2423 scopeOffset=-1;
2424 }
2425 else if ((scopeOffset=scopeName.findRev("::",scopeOffset-1))==-1)
2426 {
2427 scopeOffset=0;
2428 }
2429 } while (scopeOffset>=0);
2430
2431 }
2432 if (mn && input.scopeName.isEmpty() && mScope.isEmpty()) // Maybe a related function?
2433 {
2434 //printf("Global symbol\n");
2435 const MemberDef *fuzzy_mmd = nullptr;
2436 std::unique_ptr<ArgumentList> argList;
2437 bool hasEmptyArgs = input.args=="()";
2438
2439 if (!input.args.isEmpty())
2440 {
2441 argList = stringToArgumentList(SrcLangExt::Cpp, input.args);
2442 }
2443
2444 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2445 {
2446 const MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2447 if (!mmd->isLinkable() || (!mmd->isRelated() && !mmd->isForeign()) ||
2448 !mmd->getClassDef())
2449 {
2450 continue;
2451 }
2452
2453 if (input.args.isEmpty())
2454 {
2455 fuzzy_mmd = mmd;
2456 break;
2457 }
2458
2459 const ArgumentList &mmdAl = mmd->argumentList();
2460 if (matchArguments2(mmd->getOuterScope(),mmd->getFileDef(),&mmdAl,
2461 Doxygen::globalScope,mmd->getFileDef(),argList.get(),
2462 input.checkCV,mmd->getLanguage())
2463 )
2464 {
2465 fuzzy_mmd = mmd;
2466 break;
2467 }
2468
2469 if (!fuzzy_mmd && hasEmptyArgs)
2470 {
2471 fuzzy_mmd = mmd;
2472 }
2473 }
2474
2475 if (fuzzy_mmd && !fuzzy_mmd->isStrongEnumValue())
2476 {
2477 result.md = fuzzy_mmd;
2478 result.cd = fuzzy_mmd->getClassDef();
2479 result.found = true;
2480 goto exit;
2481 }
2482 }
2483
2484
2485 // maybe an namespace, file or group member ?
2486 //printf("Testing for global symbol scopeName='%s' mScope='%s' :: mName='%s'\n",
2487 // qPrint(scopeName), qPrint(mScope), qPrint(mName));
2488 if ((mn=Doxygen::functionNameLinkedMap->find(mName))) // name is known
2489 {
2490 //printf(" >symbol name found\n");
2491 NamespaceDef *fnd=nullptr;
2492 int scopeOffset = static_cast<int>(scopeName.length());
2493 do
2494 {
2495 QCString namespaceName = scopeName.left(scopeOffset);
2496 if (!namespaceName.isEmpty() && !mScope.isEmpty())
2497 {
2498 namespaceName+="::"+mScope;
2499 }
2500 else if (!mScope.isEmpty())
2501 {
2502 namespaceName=mScope;
2503 }
2504 //printf("Trying namespace %s\n",qPrint(namespaceName));
2505 if (!namespaceName.isEmpty() &&
2506 (fnd=Doxygen::namespaceLinkedMap->find(namespaceName)) &&
2507 fnd->isLinkable()
2508 )
2509 {
2510 //printf("Symbol inside existing namespace '%s' count=%d\n",
2511 // qPrint(namespaceName),mn->count());
2512 bool found=FALSE;
2513 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2514 {
2515 const MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2516 //printf("mmd->getNamespaceDef()=%p fnd=%p\n",
2517 // mmd->getNamespaceDef(),fnd);
2518 const MemberDef *emd = mmd->getEnumScope();
2519 if (emd && emd->isStrong())
2520 {
2521 //printf("yes match %s<->%s!\n",qPrint(mScope),qPrint(emd->localName()));
2522 if (emd->getNamespaceDef()==fnd &&
2523 rightScopeMatch(mScope,emd->localName()))
2524 {
2525 //printf("found it!\n");
2526 result.nd=fnd;
2527 result.md=mmd;
2528 found=TRUE;
2529 break;
2530 }
2531 else
2532 {
2533 result.md=nullptr;
2534 result.cd=nullptr;
2535 result.found=false;
2536 goto exit;
2537 }
2538 }
2539 else if (mmd->getOuterScope()==fnd /* && mmd->isLinkable() */ )
2540 { // namespace is found
2541 bool match=TRUE;
2542 if (!input.args.isEmpty() && input.args!="()")
2543 {
2544 const ArgumentList &mmdAl = mmd->argumentList();
2545 auto argList_p = stringToArgumentList(mmd->getLanguage(),input.args);
2546 match=matchArguments2(
2547 mmd->getOuterScope(),mmd->getFileDef(),&mmdAl,
2548 fnd,mmd->getFileDef(),argList_p.get(),
2549 input.checkCV,mmd->getLanguage());
2550 }
2551 if (match)
2552 {
2553 result.nd=fnd;
2554 result.md=mmd;
2555 found=TRUE;
2556 break;
2557 }
2558 }
2559 }
2560 if (!found && input.args=="()")
2561 // no exact match found, but if args="()" an arbitrary
2562 // member will do
2563 {
2564 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2565 {
2566 const MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2567 if (mmd->getNamespaceDef()==fnd /*&& mmd->isLinkable() */ )
2568 {
2569 result.nd=fnd;
2570 result.md=mmd;
2571 found=TRUE;
2572 break;
2573 }
2574 }
2575 }
2576 if (found)
2577 {
2578 if (!result.md->isLinkable())
2579 {
2580 result.md=nullptr; // avoid returning things we cannot link to
2581 result.nd=nullptr;
2582 result.found=false; // match found but not linkable
2583 goto exit;
2584 }
2585 else
2586 {
2587 result.gd=result.md->resolveAlias()->getGroupDef();
2588 if (result.gd && result.gd->isLinkable()) result.nd=nullptr; else result.gd=nullptr;
2589 result.found = true;
2590 goto exit;
2591 }
2592 }
2593 }
2594 else
2595 {
2596 //printf("not a namespace\n");
2597 for (const auto &mmd_p : *mn)
2598 {
2599 const MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2600 const MemberDef *tmd = mmd->getEnumScope();
2601 //printf("try member %s tmd=%s\n",qPrint(mmd->name()),tmd ? qPrint(tmd->name()) : "<none>");
2602 int ni=namespaceName.findRev("::");
2603 //printf("namespaceName=%s ni=%d\n",qPrint(namespaceName),ni);
2604 bool notInNS = tmd && ni==-1 && tmd->getNamespaceDef()==nullptr && (mScope.isEmpty() || mScope==tmd->name());
2605 bool sameNS = tmd && ni>=0 && tmd->getNamespaceDef() && namespaceName.left(ni)==tmd->getNamespaceDef()->name() && namespaceName.mid(ni+2)==tmd->name();
2606 //printf("notInNS=%d sameNS=%d\n",notInNS,sameNS);
2607 if (tmd && tmd->isStrong() && // C++11 enum class
2608 (notInNS || sameNS) &&
2609 namespaceName.length()>0 // enum is part of namespace so this should not be empty
2610 )
2611 {
2612 result.md=mmd;
2613 result.fd=mmd->getFileDef();
2614 result.gd=mmd->getGroupDef();
2615 if (result.gd && result.gd->isLinkable()) result.fd=nullptr; else result.gd=nullptr;
2616 //printf("Found scoped enum %s fd=%p gd=%p\n",
2617 // qPrint(mmd->name()),result.fd,result.gd);
2618 result.found = true;
2619 goto exit;
2620 }
2621 }
2622 }
2623 if (scopeOffset==0)
2624 {
2625 scopeOffset=-1;
2626 }
2627 else if ((scopeOffset=scopeName.findRev("::",scopeOffset-1))==-1)
2628 {
2629 scopeOffset=0;
2630 }
2631 } while (scopeOffset>=0);
2632
2633 //else // no scope => global function
2634 {
2635 std::vector<const MemberDef *> members;
2636 // search for matches with strict static checking
2637 findMembersWithSpecificName(mn,input.args,true,input.currentFile,input.checkCV,members);
2638 if (members.empty()) // nothing found
2639 {
2640 // search again without strict static checking
2641 findMembersWithSpecificName(mn,input.args,false,input.currentFile,input.checkCV,members);
2642 }
2643 //printf("found %d members\n",members.count());
2644 if (members.size()!=1 && input.args=="()")
2645 {
2646 // no exact match found, but if args="()" an arbitrary
2647 // member will do
2648 //MemberNameIterator mni(*mn);
2649 //for (mni.toLast();(md=mni.current());--mni)
2650 for (auto it = mn->rbegin(); it!=mn->rend(); ++it)
2651 {
2652 const auto &mmd_p = *it;
2653 const MemberDef *mmd = mmd_p.get();
2654 //printf("Found member '%s'\n",qPrint(mmd->name()));
2655 //printf("member is linkable mmd->name()='%s'\n",qPrint(mmd->name()));
2656 result.fd = mmd->getFileDef();
2657 result.gd = mmd->getGroupDef();
2658 const MemberDef *tmd = mmd->getEnumScope();
2659 if (
2660 (result.gd && result.gd->isLinkable()) || (result.fd && result.fd->isLinkable()) ||
2661 (tmd && tmd->isStrong())
2662 )
2663 {
2664 members.push_back(mmd);
2665 }
2666 }
2667 }
2668 //printf("found %d candidate members\n",members.count());
2669 if (!members.empty()) // at least one match
2670 {
2671 if (input.currentFile)
2672 {
2673 //printf("multiple results; pick one from file:%s\n",qPrint( currentFile->name()));
2674 for (const auto &rmd : members)
2675 {
2676 if (rmd->getFileDef() && rmd->getFileDef()->name() == input.currentFile->name())
2677 {
2678 result.md = rmd;
2679 break; // found match in the current file
2680 }
2681 }
2682 if (!result.md) // member not in the current file
2683 {
2684 result.md = members.back();
2685 }
2686 }
2687 else
2688 {
2689 result.md = members.back();
2690 }
2691 }
2692 if (result.md && (result.md->getEnumScope()==nullptr || !result.md->getEnumScope()->isStrong()))
2693 // found a matching global member, that is not a scoped enum value (or uniquely matches)
2694 {
2695 result.fd = result.md->getFileDef();
2696 result.gd = result.md->getGroupDef();
2697 //printf("fd=%p gd=%p gd->isLinkable()=%d\n",fd,gd,gd->isLinkable());
2698 if (result.gd && result.gd->isLinkable()) result.fd=nullptr; else result.gd=nullptr;
2699 result.found = true;
2700 goto exit;
2701 }
2702 }
2703 }
2704
2705exit:
2706 return result;
2707}

◆ getFileFilter()

QCString getFileFilter ( const QCString & name,
bool isSourceCode )

looks for a filter for the file name. Returns the name of the filter if there is a match for the file name, otherwise an empty string. In case inSourceCode is TRUE then first the source filter list is considered.

◆ getScopeDefs()

static bool getScopeDefs ( const QCString & docScope,
const QCString & scope,
ClassDef *& cd,
ConceptDef *& cnd,
NamespaceDef *& nd )
static

Searches for a scope definition given its name as a string via parameter scope.

The parameter docScope is a string representing the name of the scope in which the scope string was found.

The function returns TRUE if the scope is known and documented or FALSE if it is not. If TRUE is returned exactly one of the parameter cd, nd will be non-zero:

  • if cd is non zero, the scope was a class pointed to by cd.
  • if nd is non zero, the scope was a namespace pointed to by nd.

Definition at line 2777 of file util.cpp.

2779{
2780 cd=nullptr;nd=nullptr;
2781
2782 QCString scopeName=scope;
2783 //printf("getScopeDefs: docScope='%s' scope='%s'\n",qPrint(docScope),qPrint(scope));
2784 if (scopeName.isEmpty()) return FALSE;
2785
2786 bool explicitGlobalScope=FALSE;
2787 if (scopeName.at(0)==':' && scopeName.at(1)==':')
2788 {
2789 scopeName=scopeName.right(scopeName.length()-2);
2790 explicitGlobalScope=TRUE;
2791 }
2792 if (scopeName.isEmpty())
2793 {
2794 return FALSE;
2795 }
2796
2797 QCString docScopeName=docScope;
2798 int scopeOffset=explicitGlobalScope ? 0 : static_cast<int>(docScopeName.length());
2799
2800 do // for each possible docScope (from largest to and including empty)
2801 {
2802 QCString fullName=scopeName;
2803 if (scopeOffset>0) fullName.prepend(docScopeName.left(scopeOffset)+"::");
2804
2805 if (((cd=getClass(fullName)) || // normal class
2806 (cd=getClass(fullName+"-p")) // ObjC protocol
2807 ) && cd->isLinkable())
2808 {
2809 return TRUE; // class link written => quit
2810 }
2811 else if ((nd=Doxygen::namespaceLinkedMap->find(fullName)) && nd->isLinkable())
2812 {
2813 return TRUE; // namespace link written => quit
2814 }
2815 else if ((cnd=Doxygen::conceptLinkedMap->find(fullName)) && cnd->isLinkable())
2816 {
2817 return TRUE; // concept link written => quit
2818 }
2819 if (scopeOffset==0)
2820 {
2821 scopeOffset=-1;
2822 }
2823 else if ((scopeOffset=docScopeName.findRev("::",scopeOffset-1))==-1)
2824 {
2825 scopeOffset=0;
2826 }
2827 } while (scopeOffset>=0);
2828
2829 return FALSE;
2830}

◆ guessSection()

EntryType guessSection ( const QCString & name)

try to determine if name is a source or a header file name by looking at the extension. A number of variations is allowed in both upper and lower case) If anyone knows or uses another extension please let me know :-)

Definition at line 347 of file util.cpp.

348{
349 QCString n=name.lower();
350 static const std::unordered_set<std::string> sourceExt = {
351 "c","cc","cxx","cpp","c++","cppm","ccm","cxxm","c++m", // C/C++
352 "java", // Java
353 "cs", // C#
354 "m","mm", // Objective-C
355 "ii","ixx","ipp","i++","inl", // C/C++ inline
356 "xml","lex","sql" // others
357 };
358 static const std::unordered_set<std::string> headerExt = {
359 "h", "hh", "hxx", "hpp", "h++", "ixx", // C/C++ header
360 "idl", "ddl", "pidl", "ice" // IDL like
361 };
362 int lastDot = n.findRev('.');
363 if (lastDot!=-1)
364 {
365 QCString extension = n.mid(lastDot+1); // part after the last dot
366 if (sourceExt.find(extension.str())!=sourceExt.end())
367 {
368 return EntryType::makeSource();
369 }
370 if (headerExt.find(extension.str())!=headerExt.end())
371 {
372 return EntryType::makeHeader();
373 }
374 }
375 else
376 {
377 if (getLanguageFromFileName(name,SrcLangExt::Unknown) == SrcLangExt::Cpp) return EntryType::makeHeader();
378 }
379 return EntryType::makeEmpty();
380}

1832 // 'int x' matches 'int y'. A simple literal string match would treat these as different.

1834 auto dstAl = stringToArgumentList(lang,dstType.mid(i2+1));

1836 dstScope,dstFileScope,dstAl.get(),
1837 true,lang);
1838}

◆ mergeScopes()

QCString mergeScopes ( const QCString & leftScope,
const QCString & rightScope )

Merges two scope parts together. The parts may (partially) overlap. Example1: A::B and B::C will result in A::B::C
Example2: A and B will be A::B
Example3: A::B and B will be A::B

Parameters
leftScopethe left hand part of the scope.
rightScopethe right hand part of the scope.
Returns
the merged scope.

5817 if (!f)
5818 {
5819 err("could not execute filter %s\n",qPrint(filterName));
5820 return FALSE;
5821 }
5822 const int bufSize=4096;
5823 char buf[bufSize];
5824 int numRead;
5825 while ((numRead=static_cast<int>(fread(buf,1,bufSize,f)))>0)
5826 {
5827 //printf(">>>>>>>>Reading %d bytes\n",numRead);
5828 contents.append(buf,numRead);
5829 }
5830 Portable::pclose(f);
5831 Debug::print(Debug::FilterOutput, 0, "Filter output\n");
5832 Debug::print(Debug::FilterOutput,0,"-------------\n%s\n-------------\n",qPrint(contents));
5833 }
5834
5835 if (contents.size()>=2 &&
5836 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[0])==0xFF &&
5837 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[1])==0xFE // Little endian BOM
5838 ) // UCS-2LE encoded file
5839 {
5840 transcodeCharacterBuffer(fileName,contents,"UCS-2LE","UTF-8");
5841 }
5842 else if (contents.size()>=2 &&
5843 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[0])==0xFE &&
5844 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[1])==0xFF // big endian BOM
5845 ) // UCS-2BE encoded file
5846 {
5847 transcodeCharacterBuffer(fileName,contents,"UCS-2BE","UTF-8");
5848 }
5849 else if (contents.size()>=3 &&
5850 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[0])==0xEF &&
5851 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[1])==0xBB &&
5852 static_cast<uint8_t>(contents[2])==0xBF
5853 ) // UTF-8 encoded file
5854 {
5855 contents.erase(0,3); // remove UTF-8 BOM: no translation needed
5856 }
5857 else // transcode according to the INPUT_ENCODING setting
5858 {
5859 // do character transcoding if needed.
5860 transcodeCharacterBuffer(fileName,contents,getEncoding(fi),"UTF-8");
5861 }
5862
5863 filterCRLF(contents);
5864 return true;
5865}

6720{
6721 int column=0;

6723
6724 if (format == FortranFormat_Fixed) return TRUE;
6725 if (format == FortranFormat_Free) return FALSE;
6726 int tabSize=Config_getInt(TAB_SIZE);
6727 size_t sizCont;
6728
6729 sizCont = contents.length();
6730 for (size_t i=0;i<sizCont;i++)
6731 {
6732 column++;
6733
6734 switch(contents.at(i))
6735 {
6736 case '\n':
6737 column=0;
6738 skipLine=FALSE;
6739 break;
6740 case '\t':
6741 column += tabSize-1;
6742 break;
6743 case ' ':
6744 break;
6745 case '\000':
6746 return FALSE;
6747 case '#':
6748 skipLine=TRUE;
6749 break;
6750 case 'C':
6751 case 'c':
6752 if (column==1)
6753 {
6754 return !keyWordsFortranC(contents.data()+i);
6755 }
6756 // fallthrough
6757 case '*':
6758 if (column==1) return TRUE;
6759 if (skipLine) break;
6760 return FALSE;
6761 case '!':
6762 if (column!=6) skipLine=TRUE;
6763 break;
6764 default:
6765 if (skipLine) break;
6766 if (column>=7) return TRUE;
6767 return FALSE;
6768 }
6769 }
6770 return FALSE;
6771}

◆ removeAnonymousScopes()

QCString removeAnonymousScopes ( const QCString & str)

Removes all anonymous scopes from string s Possible examples: 

   "bla::@10::blep"      => "bla::blep"
   "bla::@10::@11::blep" => "bla::blep"
   "@10::blep"           => "blep"
   " @10::blep"          => "blep"
   "@9::@10::blep"       => "blep"
   "bla::@1"             => "bla"
   "bla::@1::@2"         => "bla"
   "bla @1"              => "bla"

Definition at line 170 of file util.cpp.

173 if (str.isEmpty()) return QCString(result);
174
175 // helper to check if the found delimiter starts with a colon
176 auto startsWithColon = [](const std::string &del)
177 {
178 for (size_t i=0;i<del.size();i++)
179 {
180 if (del[i]=='@') return false;
181 else if (del[i]==':') return true;
182 }
183 return false;
184 };
185
186 // helper to check if the found delimiter ends with a colon
187 auto endsWithColon = [](const std::string &del)
188 {
189 for (int i=static_cast<int>(del.size())-1;i>=0;i--)
190 {
191 if (del[i]=='@') return false;
192 else if (del[i]==':') return true;
193 }
194 return false;
195 };
196
197 static const reg::Ex re(R"([\s:]*@\d+[\s:]*)");
198 std::string s = str.str();
199 reg::Iterator iter(s,re);
200 reg::Iterator end;

205 {
206 const auto &match = *iter;
207 size_t i = match.position();
208 if (i>p) // add non-matching prefix
209 {
210 if (needsSeparator) result+="::";
211 needsSeparator=false;
212 result+=s.substr(p,i-p);
213 }
214 std::string delim = match.str();
215 needsSeparator = needsSeparator || (startsWithColon(delim) && endsWithColon(delim));
216 p = match.position()+match.length();
217 }
218 if (p<sl) // add trailing remainder
219 {
220 if (needsSeparator) result+="::";
221 result+=s.substr(p);
222 }
223 return result;
224}

QCString replaceColorMarkers ( const QCString & str)

Replaces any markers of the form ##AA in input string str by new markers of the form #AABBCC, where #AABBCC represents a valid color, based on the intensity represented by hex number AA and the current HTML_COLORSTYLE_* settings.

6876 }
6877 if (!found) // unknown marker id
6878 {

6880 p++;
6881 }
6882 }
6883 else // not a start or end marker
6884 {
6885 result+=c;
6886 p++;
6887 }
6888 }
6889 else // not a marker character
6890 {
6891 result+=c;
6892 p++;
6893 }
6894 }
6895 //printf("====\n%s\n-----\n%s\n~~~~\n",qPrint(s),qPrint(result));

6235 // compute minimum indentation over all lines
6236 const char *p=s.data();
6237 char c;
6238 int indent=0;
6239 int minIndent=1000000; // "infinite"
6240 bool searchIndent=TRUE;
6241 int tabSize=Config_getInt(TAB_SIZE);
6242 while ((c=*p++))
6243 {
6244 if (c=='\t') indent+=tabSize - (indent%tabSize);
6245 else if (c=='\n') indent=0,searchIndent=TRUE;
6246 else if (c==' ') indent++;
6247 else if (searchIndent)
6248 {
6249 searchIndent=FALSE;
6250 if (indent<minIndent) minIndent=indent;
6251 }
6252 }
6253
6254 // no indent to remove -> we're done
6255 if (minIndent==0) return s;
6256
6257 // remove minimum indentation for each line
6258 TextStream result;
6259 p=s.data();
6260 indent=0;
6261 while ((c=*p++))
6262 {
6263 if (c=='\n') // start of new line
6264 {
6265 indent=0;
6266 result << c;
6267 }
6268 else if (indent<minIndent) // skip until we reach minIndent
6269 {
6270 if (c=='\t')
6271 {
6272 int newIndent = indent+tabSize-(indent%tabSize);
6273 int i=newIndent;
6274 while (i>minIndent) // if a tab crosses the minIndent boundary fill the rest with spaces
6275 {
6276 result << ' ';
6277 i--;
6278 }
6279 indent=newIndent;
6280 }
6281 else // space
6282 {
6283 indent++;
6284 }
6285 }
6286 else // copy anything until the end of the line
6287 {
6288 result << c;
6289 }
6290 }
6291
6292 return result.str();
6293}

◆ stripIrrelevantConstVolatile()

void stripIrrelevantConstVolatile ( QCString & s)

According to the C++ spec and Ivan Vecerina:

Parameter declarations that differ only in the presence or absence of const and/or volatile are equivalent.

So the following example, show what is stripped by this routine for const. The same is done for volatile.

For Java code we also strip the "final" keyword, see bug 765070.

const T param -> T param // not relevant
const T& param -> const T& param // const needed
T* const param -> T* param // not relevant
const T* param -> const T* param // const needed

Definition at line 1508 of file util.cpp.

1509{
1510 //printf("stripIrrelevantConstVolatile(%s)=",qPrint(s));
1511 stripIrrelevantString(s,"const");
1512 stripIrrelevantString(s,"volatile");
1513 stripIrrelevantString(s,"final");
1514 //printf("%s\n",qPrint(s));
1515}
stripIrrelevantString

5324 char c;
5325 while ((c=*p))
5326 {
5327 if (c==' ' || c=='\t' || c=='\r') i++,p++;
5328 else if (c=='\\' && qstrncmp(p,"\\ilinebr",8)==0) i+=8,li=i,p+=8;

5330 else break;
5331 }
5332
5333 // search for trailing empty lines
5334 int b=l-1,bi=-1;
5335 p=s.data()+b;

5337 {

5341 else if (c=='\n') bi=b,b--,p--;
5342 else break;
5343 }
5344
5345 // return whole string if no leading or trailing lines where found

5347
5348 // return substring

4131 if (p>0 && result.at(p-1)=='>') // skip >> operator
4132 {
4133 p-=2;
4134 break;
4135 }
4136 count=1;
4137 //printf("pos < = %d\n",p);
4138 p--;
4139 bool foundMatch=false;
4140 while (p>=0 && !foundMatch)
4141 {
4142 c=result.at(p--);
4143 switch (c)
4144 {
4145 case ')':
4146 round++;
4147 break;
4148 case '(':
4149 round--;
4150 break;
4151 case '>': // ignore > inside (...) to support e.g. (sizeof(T)>0) inside template parameters
4152 if (round==0) count++;
4153 break;
4154 case '<':
4155 if (round==0)
4156 {
4157 if (p>0)
4158 {
4159 if (result.at(p-1) == '<') // skip << operator
4160 {
4161 p--;
4162 break;
4163 }
4164 }
4165 count--;
4166 foundMatch = count==0;
4167 }
4168 break;
4169 default:
4170 //printf("c=%c count=%d\n",c,count);
4171 break;
4172 }
4173 }
4174 }
4175 //printf("pos > = %d\n",p+1);
4176 break;
4177 default:
4178 p--;
4179 }
4180 }
4181 done = count==0 || skipBracket; // reparse if brackets do not match
4182 skipBracket=TRUE;
4183 }
4184 while (!done); // if < > unbalanced repeat ignoring them
4185 //printf("stripScope(%s)=%s\n",name,name);
4186 return name;
4187}

◆ stripTemplateSpecifiersFromScope()

QCString stripTemplateSpecifiersFromScope ( const QCString & fullName,
bool parentOnly,
QCString * pLastScopeStripped,
QCString scopeName,
bool allowArtificial )

Strips template specifiers from scope fullName, except those that make up specialized classes. The switch parentOnly determines whether or not a template "at the end" of a scope should be considered, e.g. with parentOnly is TRUE, A<T>::B<S> will try to strip <T> and not <S>, while parentOnly is FALSE will strip both unless A<T> or B<S> are specialized template classes.

Definition at line 4820 of file util.cpp.

4826 //printf("stripTemplateSpecifiersFromScope(name=%s,scopeName=%s)\n",qPrint(fullName),qPrint(scopeName));
4827 int i=fullName.find('<');
4828 if (i==-1) return fullName;
4829 QCString result;
4830 int p=0;
4831 int l=static_cast<int>(fullName.length());
4832 while (i!=-1)
4833 {
4834 //printf("1:result+=%s\n",qPrint(fullName.mid(p,i-p)));
4835 int e=i+1;
4836 int count=1;
4837 int round=0;
4838 while (e<l && count>0)
4839 {
4840 char c=fullName.at(e++);
4841 switch (c)
4842 {
4843 case '(': round++; break;
4844 case ')': if (round>0) round--; break;
4845 case '<': if (round==0) count++; break;
4846 case '>': if (round==0) count--; break;
4847 default:
4848 break;
4849 }
4850 }
4851 int si= fullName.find("::",e);
4852
4853 if (parentOnly && si==-1) break;
4854 // we only do the parent scope, so we stop here if needed
4855
4856 result+=fullName.mid(p,i-p);
4857 //printf(" trying %s\n",qPrint(mergeScopes(scopeName,result+fullName.mid(i,e-i))));

4859 if (cd!=nullptr && (allowArtificial || !cd->isArtificial()))
4860 {

4862 //printf(" 2:result+=%s\n",qPrint(fullName.mid(i,e-i-1)));
4863 }
4864 else if (pLastScopeStripped)
4865 {
4866 //printf(" last stripped scope '%s'\n",qPrint(fullName.mid(i,e-i)));
4867 *pLastScopeStripped=fullName.mid(i,e-i);

4869 p=e;

4874 //printf("end result=%s\n",qPrint(result));

◆ substituteTemplateArgumentsInString()

QCString substituteTemplateArgumentsInString ( const QCString & nm,
const ArgumentList & formalArgs,
const ArgumentList * actualArgs )

Substitutes any occurrence of a formal argument from argument list formalArgs in name by the corresponding actual argument in argument list actualArgs. The result after substitution is returned as a string. The argument name is used to prevent recursive substitution.

3761 if (p)
3762 {
3763 char c;
3764 while ((c=*p++))
3765 {
3766 if (c=='_') // 2 or 3 character escape
3767 {
3768 switch (*p)
3769 {
3770 case '_': result+=c; p++; break; // __ -> '_'
3771 case '1': result+=':'; p++; break; // _1 -> ':'
3772 case '2': result+='/'; p++; break; // _2 -> '/'
3773 case '3': result+='<'; p++; break; // _3 -> '<'
3774 case '4': result+='>'; p++; break; // _4 -> '>'
3775 case '5': result+='*'; p++; break; // _5 -> '*'
3776 case '6': result+='&'; p++; break; // _6 -> '&'
3777 case '7': result+='|'; p++; break; // _7 -> '|'
3778 case '8': result+='.'; p++; break; // _8 -> '.'
3779 case '9': result+='!'; p++; break; // _9 -> '!'
3780 case '0': // 3 character escape
3781 switch (*(p+1))
3782 {
3783 case '0': result+=','; p+=2; break; // _00 -> ','
3784 case '1': result+=' '; p+=2; break; // _01 -> ' '
3785 case '2': result+='{'; p+=2; break; // _02 -> '{'
3786 case '3': result+='}'; p+=2; break; // _03 -> '}'
3787 case '4': result+='?'; p+=2; break; // _04 -> '?'
3788 case '5': result+='^'; p+=2; break; // _05 -> '^'
3789 case '6': result+='%'; p+=2; break; // _06 -> '%'
3790 case '7': result+='('; p+=2; break; // _07 -> '('
3791 case '8': result+=')'; p+=2; break; // _08 -> ')'
3792 case '9': result+='+'; p+=2; break; // _09 -> '+'
3793 case 'a': result+='='; p+=2; break; // _0a -> '='
3794 case 'b': result+='$'; p+=2; break; // _0b -> '$'
3795 case 'c': result+='\\'; p+=2; break;// _0c -> '\'
3796 case 'd': result+='@'; p+=2; break; // _0d -> '@'
3797 case 'e': result+=']'; p+=2; break; // _0e -> ']'
3798 case 'f': result+='['; p+=2; break; // _0f -> '['
3799 case 'g': result+='#'; p+=2; break; // _0g -> '#'
3800 case 'h': result+='"'; p+=2; break; // _0h -> '"'
3801 case 'i': result+='~'; p+=2; break; // _0i -> '~'
3802 case 'j': result+='\''; p+=2; break;// _0j -> '\'
3803 case 'k': result+=';'; p+=2; break; // _0k -> ';'
3804 case 'l': result+='`'; p+=2; break; // _0l -> '`'
3805 default: // unknown escape, just pass underscore character as-is
3806 result+=c;
3807 break;
3808 }
3809 break;
3810 default:
3811 if (!caseSenseNames && c>='a' && c<='z') // lower to upper case escape, _a -> 'A'
3812 {
3813 result+=static_cast<char>(toupper(*p));
3814 p++;
3815 }
3816 else // unknown escape, pass underscore character as-is
3817 {
3818 result+=c;
3819 }
3820 break;
3821 }
3822 }
3823 else // normal character; pass as is
3824 {
3825 result+=c;
3826 }
3827 }
3828 }
3829 return result;
3830}

References QCString::data(), getCaseSenseNames(), and QCString::isEmpty().
